outcomes.statement: Explain alignment stack and its effect on phonation
Demonstrate wall drill alignment with consistent head/neck position
Perform a daily posture check before singing

steps.title: The Alignment Stack
steps.body: Stand against a wall: head touches lightly, shoulders touch, hips touch. Step away while maintaining this stacked position. Your ears should align over your shoulders, shoulders over hips, hips over ankles. The spine is long but not rigid. The jaw hangs free, not jutted forward.

steps.title: Wall Drill
steps.body: Stand in the alignment position against the wall for 60 seconds. Then step away and sing a comfortable note on 'oo'. Maintain the alignment. Do this 3 times. Notice: does your chin lift when you sing higher? That breaks alignment. Keep the head level.

steps.title: Daily Posture Check
steps.body: Every morning for 7 days, do the wall drill before singing. After 7 days, you should be able to find alignment without the wall. Test: sing a scale from lowest comfortable to highest comfortable note. If you feel tension in your neck or jaw at any point, reset your alignment and start the scale again.

steps.title: Pro Tip
steps.body: Professional singers check their alignment before every performance. Some use a mirror, some use the wall, some just feel it. The key is consistency — your body should default to this position without thinking.
